hard.next_step: 29th of June - Confidence : 90% Good call with Émeline she did not came back on Merlin for I Contract to be put as option instead of core module which would have a significant impact (50K if we consider discount) on TCV (let's see if Florence will insist or not) we are almost there for OF1, Advanced support options to be added (good news potential additional revenue ! ) and 2 minor typos / format to be changed. If we can have this by tomorrow that would be great. For closing plan we are aligned target is still mid of July. 24th of June - Confidence : 80% BAFO sent today Negotiation meeting scheduled on 25th of June Second round of legal comments should be received from Publicis by 25th of June Questionnaire to finalize SoW / OF2 should be received by 25th of June => Close plan - Final version for final review of contractual package to be sent by Zycus on 29th / 30th of June => Comments / last negotiation calls from 1st of July till 10th of July => Signature target date : 15th of July => Project start last week of July 12th of June - Confidence : 80% Publicis : Positive => Émeline (purchaser) super pushy - already 4 version of OF1 - we included IAnalyze as option could significantly increase the deal size / Supplier creation process launched - Legal : OK were supposed to send first remark this week will be more probably early next week (Émeline is off, she will be back Monday) so not perfectly on time but ok for legal Questionnable (not negative at this stage) => Willingness of Stephen Cook (Head of procurement performance (so in charge of processes and tools) and Paras (external consultant important in tech validation) to move forward as I sent 3 reminders asking them to schedule a WS to finalize contract / John one => 0 Feedback. Action : I already sent them a 4th reminder with questionnaire from Hrishikesh for them to prepare this WS if no answer by Monday afternoon we need to escalate (direct call to Florence, ask for a call with Aatish...others.. to be discussed) 4st of june - Confidence : 80% We answered with our completed OF1 to 3 points. NS and agreed close plan 5th of june : send a new OF1 to answer to Emeline requests to reduce the budget for FY26 - We should have an acceptable answer Starting 8th of June : Start legal discusssion on MSA (no clear close plan / no contact with Legal at this stage) 8th of June : Publicis following our presentation on 2nd of June should come back to us with additional questions to ensure that the high level vision of our champion Florence is really aligned with their contractual processes (that's the main risk) Between 8th nd 12th of June : Schedule a 90 minutes WS to be able to create a SoW (OF2) to be sent around 15th of June Joined target shared by Publicis and Zycus : Start the project in July for a go live (quick win) before end of FY26 => Need to finalize agreement end of June / First week of July (at this stage I let our close date in July) => Secure this close plan with a Top Management Call Aatish and Florence / Need first to have their questions and have a good knowledge about potential blockers so this call will not be before 15th of June. 1st of June - Confidence : 80% Starting Contractual discussion at the end of our call today. 4 points to be addressed. Still need to Come back with OF1 completed asap (with clear answer on 4 points) Schedule a SoW WS in the coming days / Prior to this Publicis to come back with additional questions that could oblige us to make a second presentations based on their uses cases - Close plan not entirely clear depending of the list of questions we will receive in the coming days / Florence as champion confirmed her willingness to close asap and have a quick win (solution in production) before end of 26. => See how we will have to involve Aatish for a call with Florence to ensure our full support (at this stage I think we need first to have their feedback so additional questions and us to send OF1 before to have an efficient call with clear take away). 26th May - Vendor Selected 21st of May - Confidence : 80% Next steps - Shared with Publicis Contract In order to send you two order forms - one for the subscription, OF1, and one for the implementation, OF2, we would need to organize a short 90-minute workshop with your technical team. The objective would be to validate all assumptions and ensure that OF2 is fully aligned with Publicis’ expectations. This workshop can be organized as soon as possible. Once it has taken place, we will need four working days to finalize OF1 and OF2 and send them to you for review. A possible retro-planning could therefore be: Workshop next week OF1 and OF2 sent by 5 June for review Target to finalize the agreement before the end of June Project start in July Go-live before the end of 2026 11th of May - Confidence : 40% Assumptions for pricing sent / Need confirmation from CPO (Florence) + input on number of existing contracts PS Estimation needs to be done by 15th of may Proposal to be sent on 15th of May / WS to refine implementation time line and assumptions to be scheduled end of May. First version of OF1 / OF2 mid of June / Negotiation process : Target date signature mid of Juy.
hard.ais_why:
• SF Description identifies Florence as 'a strong CPO champion' from PwC where Zycus deployed ‚Äî a named CPO who has prior Zycus experience and is now driving the Publicis evaluation. Product scope includes Merlin and Merlin Intake within the CLM/iRequest stack (iContract, iRequest, iSupplier, Merlin, Merlin Intake). The differentiation narrative names AI-driven sourcing, autonomous negotiation, and automated compliance.
• Qualified stage with proposal sent 1 May 2026 and target signature mid-July. 4 prior Avoma meetings (May/Sep 2025) with Cedric Elmaleh predate Florence's CPO arrival and are not directly relevant. The current cycle is partner-led (Bertrand intro) with no recorded Florence call yet on this specific opp.
• Florence's compelling event per Description is a year-end mandate to demonstrate procurement impact, not specifically AI; the deal is structured as a quick-win CLM/Intake replication of the PwC blueprint. Pushed UP from prior 5 to 6 based on the rich Description + Merlin in scope + named CPO champion with prior Zycus experience. Score 6 reflects Florence's potential AI championship without explicit AI-led mandate captured yet.
hard.primary_competitor: Status quo / internal FY26 budget de-scoping (no external rival)

north_star_verdict.headline: Healthy sole-source deal in final contracting: MSA legal resolved, NDA executed, functional doc received and the stalled SoW workshop held today (2026-07-06), but OF2/SoW is still unbuilt, the OneTrust security declaration is 6 of 17 answered, and Merlin core-vs-option is unsettled with only 9 days to the buyer's 2026-07-15 signature target.
north_star_verdict.math: 9 days to close (2026-07-15, today 2026-07-06). Opp created 2026-05-07; Vendor Selected reached 2026-05-26 (41 days in stage, normal for enterprise contract execution). Buyer's own signature target is 2026-07-15 and both sides remain aligned ('target is still mid of July', Next Step 2026-06-29). Overdue dependencies cleared this cycle: MSA legal fully resolved (2026-06-29/30), Publicis returned a near-executable revised document (2026-06-26), the completed functional questionnaire arrived (2026-07-01), the NDA is fully executed (2026-06-29), and the SoW review call with Stephen Cook and Paras that stalled 19 days was booked and is being held today (event 'Zycus- PG review', 2026-07-06). Remaining before signature: build and finalise OF2/SoW off today's call, settle Merlin core-vs-option (~$50K TCV), complete the OneTrust vendor security declaration (6 of 17 answered as of 2026-06-30), and run final negotiation (planned 2026-07-01 to 2026-07-10). CloseDate nudged 2026-07-13 to 2026-07-15 on 2026-06-29 (minor). The date is aggressive but plausible; if OF2 finalisation or the security declaration slips past 2026-07-10 the signature slips past 2026-07-15.
evidence: Next Step 2026-06-29 (Confidence 90%): 'Good call with Emeline ... we are almost there for OF1, Advanced support options to be added (good news potential additional revenue!) and 2 minor typos / format to be changed ... For closing plan we are aligned target is still mid of July.'
Email 2026-06-29: 'All legal clauses have now been resolved, and I have also updated Exhibit 2 related to the VOC process. I will let you perform a final review.' MSA effectively closed.
Email 2026-06-26 (from Emeline Le Foyer): 'Please find attached our revised document ... to get closer to executable version.' Second round of legal comments received.

Email 2026-07-01 (from Emeline Le Foyer): 'Please find attached the document completed on the functional part ... Do you have availability on Friday or Monday to discuss it?' SoW functional questionnaire received.

Event 2026-07-06 'Zycus- PG review' and Task 2026-07-01 confirmation call booking: the previously stalled SoW/process review with Stephen Cook and Paras is now happening ('allow Stephen and Paras to prepare').
NDA fully executed 2026-06-29 (Adobe Sign, all parties finished).
OneTrust vendor security declaration only 6 of 17 questions answered (Praveen Seemundun, 2026-06-30); PASS anti-bribery assessment completed 2026-07-02.
LastActivityDate 2026-07-06; two-way email cadence daily through 2026-07-02 (buyer highly engaged).

sources: SF pain fields / MEDDPICC Identify pain
metrics.narrative: The buyer has articulated clear qualitative operational pain but no quantified value case has been captured. The pain is process inefficiency: manual email-based contract validation, no dashboard, and no easy visibility into contract status or next steps (SF pain fields, Next Step 2026-06-04). Florence Tinsley-Roy's mandate is a year-end push to demonstrate procurement impact with a quick-win go-live before end of FY26 (AIS_Why, Next Step 2026-06-01), which implies urgency but not a dollar figure. No contract cycle-time, headcount, or compliance-cost number has been stated. With the $250K commercials now being defended against a ~$50K Merlin de-scope, anchoring the price to a savings and speed number in a short ROI validation with Publicis procurement/finance remains the open move.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: Florence Tinsley-Roy, Chief Procurement Officer, Decision Maker, PRIMARY
Email 2026-06-24 Updated Commercial Proposal addressed to Florence and Emeline
Email 2026-06-24 John Woodcock (VP EMEA) catch-up with Florence
economic_buyer.narrative: Florence Tinsley-Roy, Chief Procurement Officer, is the Economic Buyer and Decision Maker (OpportunityContactRole, PRIMARY, Role=Decision Maker) and the named budget owner. She made the sole-source vendor selection on her prior Zycus/PwC track record and carries a year-end mandate to show procurement impact. Her authority showed when the Updated Commercial Proposal was addressed directly to her (Email 2026-06-24) and Zycus VP EMEA John Woodcock arranged to catch up with her the next day ('Looking forward to catching up tomorrow', Email 2026-06-24). Day-to-day commercial and legal negotiation is run by Emeline Le Foyer (Global IT Procurement Director), but final sign-off on the $250K package and on keeping Merlin as core still needs Florence's explicit word, which is the last authority gate before signature.

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are fit-and-speed rather than a competitive bake-off, because Zycus was selected sole-source. The buyer is validating that Zycus's high-level vision matches their contractual processes: 'additional questions to ensure that the high level vision of our champion Florence is really aligned with their contractual processes (that's the main risk)' (Next Step 2026-06-08). Concrete criteria confirmed across the cycle are centralised CLM (iContract), request/approval automation (iRequest), supplier onboarding, Merlin-for-iContract AI value, a completed functional/SoW questionnaire (Email 2026-07-01), advanced support options, and a Merlin-core-vs-option cost decision. The forcing criterion is the FY26 quick-win go-live, which is why the buyer is compressing evaluation into a fast contracting path.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a compressed sole-source contracting process the deal team can read directly from the call and email sequence: SoW workshop to validate assumptions, then OF1 (subscription order form) and OF2 (implementation SoW) issued for review, legal MSA redlines in parallel, vendor compliance/onboarding via the relevant stakeholder:Sources, and a targeted 2026-07-15 signature. Approval widened as it progressed: Emeline Le Foyer drives daily execution, Stephen Cook (Head of Procurement Performance, owns processes and tools) and Paras (external tech-validation consultant) were pulled in for the SoW/process review (held today, 2026-07-06), and Florence remains the sign-off authority. The one process wobble was a 19-day stall waiting on Stephen Cook and Paras to schedule the workshop (Next Step 2026-06-12, '0 Feedback ... we need to escalate'), now cleared by today's review call.

paper_process.narrative: Paperwork is well advanced but not finished. The MSA had all legal clauses resolved with Exhibit 2 (VOC process) updated and handed back for final review (Email 2026-06-29); the NDA is fully executed (Adobe Sign, 2026-06-29); OF1 (subscription order form) is near-final with only minor typos and advanced-support additions outstanding (Next Step 2026-06-29). OF2 (implementation SoW) is the remaining artifact, to be built from today's 2026-07-06 functional review and, per Zycus contracting, signed separately by the AVP Global Delivery. Vendor compliance runs through Publicis Re:Sources (Praveen Seemundun): the anti-bribery / PASS self-assessment was signed 2026-06-17 and completed 2026-07-02 and Ariba supplier-network onboarding is in progress, but the OneTrust vendor security declaration is only 6 of 17 questions answered (2026-06-30) with earlier access issues for Zycus legal. As a France/W. Europe deal, a purchase order is not a required gate. The forcing function is the buyer's own 2026-07-15 signature target and late-July go-live.

sources: SF pain field / MEDDPICC Identify pain
identify_pain.narrative: The confirmed pain is a broken manual contract-management process owned by procurement: 'Actual contractual process is a mess / Email validation / No dashboard / No possibility to know easily what are the next steps' (SF pain field / Next Step 2026-06-04). The remedy scoped is iContract for centralised CLM, iRequest for request/approval automation, iSupplier for supplier onboarding, and Merlin for contract intelligence and visibility. The pain owner is Florence Tinsley-Roy (CPO) and her procurement team, and her year-end mandate to show impact with a quick win before end of FY26 gives the pain a clock. It is confirmed but qualitative; the financial magnitude has not been quantified.

champion.narrative: There is a two-tier champion structure, strengthening this cycle. Florence Tinsley-Roy (CPO, Decision Maker) is the strategic champion: she selected Zycus sole-source, has prior Zycus deployment experience from PwC, and set the FY26 quick-win urgency. Emeline Le Foyer (Global IT Procurement Director) is the operational champion who drives the deal daily, described as 'super pushy' with 'already 4 version of OF1' (Next Step 2026-06-12), handled the BAFO, and pulls Zycus for calls (requested Friday/Monday call, Email 2026-07-01). What strengthened this cycle: the internal blockers cleared (MSA legal resolved, functional doc delivered, and Stephen Cook and Paras finally re-engaged for today's 2026-07-06 review). What is still missing is Florence's explicit sign-off on the final commercial package and on keeping Merlin as core rather than an option.

competition.narrative: There is no external competitor: Publicis selected Zycus sole-source with no RFP, on a partner introduction (Bertrand) and Florence's prior Zycus/PwC track record. The only competitive pressure is internal. First, status-quo / do-nothing risk, which is low given the stated pain and Florence's year-end quick-win mandate. Second, and more current, internal de-scoping: Emeline is pushing to move Merlin for iContract from core module to an option, a roughly $50K TCV swing (Next Step 2026-06-29) that also erodes the AI differentiation. We hold value by keeping Merlin core, tying its contract-intelligence and agentic-intake value to the confirmed visibility pain and the quick-win go-live, and getting Florence to bless the scope before OF1 is frozen.

deal_movement.summary: Contracting is converging: this cycle cleared MSA legal, executed the NDA, received the completed functional questionnaire, and held the previously-stalled SoW/process review with Stephen Cook and Paras today (2026-07-06). OF2/SoW build, the Merlin core-vs-option decision, and the OneTrust security declaration are the remaining items before the 2026-07-15 signature.

items.change: SoW / process review call with Stephen Cook and Paras held (previously stalled 19 days)
Completed functional questionnaire received from Emeline Le Foyer for OF2/SoW build
MSA all legal clauses resolved, Exhibit 2 (VOC) updated, returned for final review
NDA fully executed (Adobe Sign, all parties)
Publicis returned near-executable revised contract document
Amount raised 230K to 250K; forecast moved Best Case to Commit

competitive_position.summary: Sole-source: Publicis selected Zycus without an RFP, on a partner introduction and Florence Tinsley-Roy's prior PwC deployment, so there is no external competitor to displace. The single strongest current threat is internal value erosion, not a rival: de-scoping Merlin for iContract from core to an option, a roughly $50K TCV swing under active negotiation (Next Step 2026-06-29). Status-quo / manual-process inertia is the secondary threat and is low given the confirmed pain and Florence's year-end quick-win mandate. No external competitor surfaced in any source this cycle; we protect value by keeping Merlin core, anchored to the contract-visibility pain and the FY26 go-live.

best_practice_check.summary: A healthy sole-source deal 9 days from signature; the win now hinges on execution discipline, not competition.
flags: Lock Florence Tinsley-Roy's explicit sign-off on the $250K package and Merlin-as-core before OF1 is frozen, protecting ~$50K TCV.
Drive the OneTrust security declaration to 17 of 17 this week, the one compliance gate that can silently push signature past 2026-07-15.
Convert today's 2026-07-06 process review with the relevant stakeholder and Paras straight into a finalised OF2/SoW, keeping the two once-silent process owners engaged.

items.name: Florence Tinsley-Roy
items.title: Chief Procurement Officer
items.risk: Light direct footprint on execution detail; explicit sign-off on the $250K package and on Merlin-as-core not yet confirmed.
items.sentiment: Positive (strategic champion, prior Zycus/PwC deployment, made the sole-source selection, FY26 quick-win mandate)

champion_strength.summary: Two-tier champion structure, strengthening this cycle. Florence Tinsley-Roy (CPO, Decision Maker) is the strategic champion who selected Zycus sole-source on her PwC track record and set the FY26 quick-win mandate; Emeline Le Foyer (Global IT Procurement Director) is the operational champion driving daily execution, negotiation, and OF1 iterations, and pulls Zycus for calls. The internal blockers that once flagged champion risk cleared this cycle: MSA legal resolved, functional doc delivered, and Stephen Cook and Paras re-engaged for today's 2026-07-06 review. What remains is Florence's explicit sign-off on the final commercial package and on keeping Merlin core.
champion_strength.source: OpportunityContactRole (Florence, Decision Maker); SF activity contact (Emeline Le Foyer); Next Step 2026-06-29; Email 2026-07-01

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI is scoped (Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI in the product stack) and is a genuine differentiator on the PwC blueprint, but the Merlin-for-iContract core-vs-option debate risks demoting the AI value to a paid option and diluting positioning. The move is to keep Merlin core and land its contract-intelligence value with Florence.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 6 / Moderate: Merlin and Merlin Intake are in scope and the differentiation narrative names AI-driven sourcing and automated compliance, but Florence's compelling event is a year-end procurement-impact mandate rather than an explicit AI-led mandate, and no transcribed call this cycle re-confirms AI appetite. The internal push to make Merlin an option is a mild under-rating signal on the AI story.

items.detail: FY26 budget pressure driving a Merlin core-to-option de-scope, ~$50K TCV erosion.
OneTrust vendor security declaration only 6 of 17 answered; access issues delayed completion.
OF2/SoW still unbuilt with 9 days to the 2026-07-15 signature target; final negotiation window 1-10 July is tight.
Florence Tinsley-Roy's explicit sign-off on the final package and Merlin scope not yet captured in writing.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is live and strong: daily two-way email cadence through 2026-07-02, LastActivityDate 2026-07-06, the buyer requesting calls, and both sides holding the 2026-07-15 signature target. Not cooling.
items: Buyer pull: Emeline requested a Friday/Monday call (Email 2026-07-01).
Buyer-named signature date held: 'target is still mid of July' (Next Step 2026-06-29).
Once-stalled process owners (Stephen Cook, Paras) re-engaged for the 2026-07-06 review.

scope_change.to: $250K with advanced support and IAnalyze / IAnalyze offered as options
scope_change.detail: Amount rose 230K to 250K on 2026-06-01 and advanced support plus IAnalyze were introduced as optional add-ons (potential additional revenue). Counter-pressure exists via the Merlin core-to-option debate (~$50K), but net scope has grown, not narrowed.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: We are the sole-source selected vendor with a CPO champion (Florence Tinsley-Roy) and a super-engaged operational champion (Emeline Le Foyer) racing us to a buyer-set 2026-07-15 signature; the only things between us and the win are building OF2/SoW off today's review, finishing the OneTrust security declaration, and locking Florence on price and Merlin-as-core.

win_position.text: High and held below stage ceiling: sole-source selection with a CPO decision-maker and no external rival earns a strong win read, but we hold it back from the top because Florence's explicit sign-off on the $250K package is not yet in writing and Merlin scope is contested.
Zycus was chosen without an RFP on Florence Tinsley-Roy's prior Zycus/PwC deployment and a partner introduction, so there is no competitor to displace (AIS_Why; SF competitor fields empty).

FY26 budget pressure is pushing Merlin for iContract from core to an option, a ~$50K TCV erosion that also dilutes the AI differentiation ('let's see if Florence will insist or not', Next Step 2026-06-29).
The OneTrust vendor security declaration is only 6 of 17 answered (Praveen Seemundun, 2026-06-30) and OF2/SoW is still unbuilt, either of which can push signature past the 2026-07-15 target.

deal_momentum.text: This cycle cleared MSA legal ('All legal clauses have now been resolved', 2026-06-29), executed the NDA (Adobe Sign, 2026-06-29), and received the completed functional questionnaire (2026-07-01).
The SoW/process review that stalled 19 days on Stephen Cook and Paras was booked and held today (event 'Zycus- PG review', 2026-07-06).
The compressed 1-10 July final-negotiation window leaves almost no slack against the 2026-07-15 signature if any redline reopens.

customer_commitment.text: Both sides hold the buyer-named signature date: 'For closing plan we are aligned target is still mid of July' (Next Step 2026-06-29).
Operational champion Emeline Le Foyer is pulling the deal ('super pushy - already 4 version of OF1', 2026-06-12; requested a call, 2026-07-01) and daily two-way email ran through 2026-07-02.
Final buyer commitment still depends on Florence Tinsley-Roy's explicit CPO sign-off, which has not yet been captured in writing.

deal_risk.text: OF2/SoW must be built and finalised off today's 2026-07-06 review within days to keep the 2026-07-15 signature credible.
The OneTrust security declaration (6 of 17) and earlier access issues are the one compliance gate that can silently slip the close.
Merlin core-to-option de-scope threatens ~$50K of TCV and the AI value story if left unresolved before OF1 is frozen.

customer_commitment.evidence: Buyer-set 2026-07-15 signature held on both sides; champion pull and daily cadence, pending Florence's written sign-off.

deal_risk.evidence: OF2/SoW unbuilt and OneTrust security 6 of 17 with 9 days to target; Merlin de-scope pressure.

customer_preference.evidence: Sole-source selection of Zycus without an RFP on Florence's PwC track record; Emeline 'super pushy' driving OF1 and requesting calls; buyer holding the mid-July signature target.

business_case.evidence: Clear qualitative pain (manual contract process, no dashboard, no visibility) and a FY26 quick-win mandate, but no quantified ROI / savings / cycle-time figure captured.

items.trigger: Emeline pushing Merlin from core to option, ~$50K TCV swing 'let's see if Florence will insist or not'.
items.expected_effect: Protects ~$50K TCV and the AI value story, and removes the last authority gate before signature.

items.expected_effect: Delivers the last missing contract artifact so the 2026-07-15 signature stays credible.

items.expected_effect: Closes the compliance gate that can silently push signature past mid-July.

items.expected_effect: Secures executive sponsorship of the mid-July signature and the FY26 go-live commitment.

items.trigger: Joined target: start project in July, go-live before end of FY26.
items.expected_effect: Turns the signed contract straight into a scheduled kickoff, cementing the quick-win narrative.

expansion_context.note: New-business sole-source deal; no sibling Closed-Won opp on Publicis Groupe evidenced this cycle.

contributions.evidence: customer requested next meeting (from call evidence).
Verdict flags close-date risk this sweep.

commentary.win_position: Win 92 is carried by preference strength +1.00 (weight 20); champion strength +1.00 (weight 15). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 68 (moving forward): seniority rising (from call evidence). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 25 reflects real customer investment: Direct economic-buyer access recorded.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 0: no break-risk was observed in the swept evidence. Coverage is thin, so read this as 'nothing seen yet,' not 'nothing there.'
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 89 is anchored on the stage win (92), nudged by commitment 25 and momentum 68, and dragged by risk 0. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, exec and champion, commercial, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

cro_panel.header: Publicis Groupe — Vendor Selected · $250K · closes 15 Jul
cro_panel.intro: We are the sole-source selected vendor with a CPO champion (Florence Tinsley-Roy) and a super-engaged operational champion (Emeline Le Foyer) racing us to a buyer-set 2026-07-15 signature; the only things between us and the win are building OF2/SoW off today's review, finishing the OneTrust security declaration, and locking Florence on price and Merlin-as-core.

blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.text: High and held below stage ceiling: sole-source selection with a CPO decision-maker and no external rival earns a strong win read, but we hold it back from the top because Florence's explicit sign-off on the $250K package is not yet in writing and Merlin scope is contested.
Zycus was chosen without an RFP on Florence Tinsley-Roy's prior Zycus/PwC deployment and a partner introduction, so there is no competitor to displace (AIS_Why; SF competitor fields empty).

FY26 budget pressure is pushing Merlin for iContract from core to an option, a ~$50K TCV erosion that also dilutes the AI differentiation ('let's see if Florence will insist or not', Next Step 2026-06-29).
The OneTrust vendor security declaration is only 6 of 17 answered (Praveen Seemundun, 2026-06-30) and OF2/SoW is still unbuilt, either of which can push signature past the 2026-07-15 target.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Vendor Selected' anchors near 72. Win signals and momentum lift the read to 92.

bullets.text: This cycle cleared MSA legal ('All legal clauses have now been resolved', 2026-06-29), executed the NDA (Adobe Sign, 2026-06-29), and received the completed functional questionnaire (2026-07-01).
The SoW/process review that stalled 19 days on Stephen Cook and Paras was booked and held today (event 'Zycus- PG review', 2026-07-06).
The compressed 1-10 July final-negotiation window leaves almost no slack against the 2026-07-15 signature if any redline reopens.

blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.

bullets.text: OF2/SoW must be built and finalised off today's 2026-07-06 review within days to keep the 2026-07-15 signature credible.
The OneTrust security declaration (6 of 17) and earlier access issues are the one compliance gate that can silently slip the close.
Merlin core-to-option de-scope threatens ~$50K of TCV and the AI value story if left unresolved before OF1 is frozen.

blocks.footer: Competitive picture: Sole-source: Publicis selected Zycus without an RFP, on a partner introduction and Florence Tinsley-Roy's prior PwC deployment, so there is no external competitor to displace.

blocks.title: Customer commitment
blocks.read: Light — they haven't committed much yet.

bullets.text: Both sides hold the buyer-named signature date: 'For closing plan we are aligned target is still mid of July' (Next Step 2026-06-29).
Operational champion Emeline Le Foyer is pulling the deal ('super pushy - already 4 version of OF1', 2026-06-12; requested a call, 2026-07-01) and daily two-way email ran through 2026-07-02.
Final buyer commitment still depends on Florence Tinsley-Roy's explicit CPO sign-off, which has not yet been captured in writing.

blocks.title: What moves it forward
items: Lock Florence's written sign-off on the $250K package and Merlin-as-core. (by 10 Jul)
Convert today's process review into a finalised OF2/SoW for buyer review. (by 10 Jul)
Complete the OneTrust vendor security declaration to 17 of 17 with Praveen. (by 9 Jul)

calls_omitted.call: Zycus Procurement AI World Tour: Paris Exclusive Dinner Event (2025-09-30)
calls_omitted.reason: networking/event, no transcript and predates the current Florence-led cycle

gaps: No transcribed buyer Avoma call in the current cycle; the read is grounded in the Next Step trail, field history, and the Salesforce email/activity thread (Emeline Le Foyer, Praveen Seemundun, Florence Tinsley-Roy).
No quantified ROI / savings figure captured for the business case.
validation gate: a named person in a to-do/action item is in no Salesforce contact role, Avoma attendee list, or active-user roster and is anchored to no source (offending: 'Stephen Cook')
